name: Coq Au Vin Blanc - Mccalls Cooking School
recipeIngredient: 3 1/2 pounds chicken, cut into quarters (3 to 3 1/2 pounds)
4 small carrots, peeled and cut diagonally into 1 1/2-inch pieces
1 package (8 ounces) frozen sugar snap peas
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large skillet over medium-high heat, melt the butter.
2. Add chicken pieces skin side down and brown well. Using tongs, turn chicken pieces to brown on all sides. Transfer chicken pieces to a large baking dish. Set aside.
3. Preheat oven to 350°F. To drippings that remain in skillet, add onions, carrots, and garlic. Saute over medium-high heat until onions and carrots are lightly browned.
4. Add brandy to skillet. Holding skillet with a pot holder, carefully but quickly ignite the brandy. Shake skillet gently until the flames subside.
5. Add wine, parsley, bay leaf, salt, pepper, thyme, and ground cloves.
6. Increase heat to high and bring the carrot mixture to boiling, stirring frequently. Remove skillet from heat.
7. Using a large spoon, transfer the carrot mixture to the chicken in the baking dish.
8. Cover baking dish with lid or aluminum foil. Place on center rack of oven and bake for 30 minutes.
9. Uncover dish and distribute sugar snap peas evenly over the chicken and carrot mixture.
10. Cover baking dish and bake for an additional 30 minutes, or until juices run clear when chicken is pierced with a fork and vegetables are tender. Remove bay leaf. Transfer to a decorative serving platter.
RED WINE VARIATION:
To make a more classic version of this chicken dish, substitute a hearty red wine, such as Burgundy, Beaujolais, or Chianti, for the white wine. Add 1/2 pound tiny white mushrooms to the skillet when the carrots are added and stir 1/2 tablespoon tomato paste into the carrot mixture just before the chicken goes into the oven.
